detail.assertion_text: The output contains all 11 required sections including INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER'S AXE, and NEGOTIATION POSITION
detail.evidence: Output includes all 11 sections: DEAL VITALS, VALUATION ASSESSMENT, INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY, SELLER-SIDE DECODING, SELLER'S AXE, ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE, RED FLAGS, GREEN FLAGS, DUE DILIGENCE PRIORITY QUESTIONS, NEGOTIATION POSITION, and BROKER'S VERDICT

detail.assertion_text: The output applies laundromat-specific multiples (unattended self-service range of 2.5x-4.0x) and correctly calculates the SDE multiple as approximately 3.67x ($385,000 / $105,000)
detail.evidence: Section 1 (DEAL VITALS) and Section 2 (VALUATION ASSESSMENT) correctly calculate SDE multiple as 3.67x and benchmark against 2.5x-4.0x range for unattended self-service laundromats, rating it as 'FAIR -- upper end'

detail.assertion_text: The output flags coin-only operation as a critical due diligence concern because revenue cannot be independently verified, and recommends cross-checking with utility bills
detail.evidence: Section 2 extensively discusses coin-only revenue verification risk. Section 4 (SELLER-SIDE DECODING) explicitly states 'Revenue is unverifiable. Coin-only laundromats produce no transaction logs.' Section 9 includes specific question: 'Can you share 24 months of utility bills -- water, gas, electric, and sewer?' with note 'Water usage correlates directly with wash cycles.'

detail.assertion_text: The output analyzes the lease (4 years remaining + one 5-year renewal = 9 years max) against equipment payback period
detail.evidence: Section 3 (INTRINSIC VALUE & MARGIN OF SAFETY) includes 'The 20-Year Test' subsection: 'The current lease has 4 years remaining plus one 5-year renewal option = 9 years maximum.' Section 4 also flags: 'Four years remaining is short. Need to understand: What is the rent? What are the escalation terms?'

detail.assertion_text: The output flags 'most replaced within last 5 years' as vague language requiring serial numbers and maintenance logs for every machine
detail.evidence: Section 4 (SELLER-SIDE DECODING) states: 'Equipment condition details are vague. "Most replaced within last 5 years" is deliberately imprecise... Without serial numbers and maintenance logs, this claim is unverifiable.' Section 9 includes DQ #2: 'I'm curious about the equipment -- do you have a detailed inventory with serial numbers, brands, and installation dates for each machine?'

detail.assertion_text: The 20% seller financing at 7% from a 68-year-old retiring owner is recognized as a positive confidence signal
detail.evidence: Section 5 (SELLER'S AXE) identifies seller as 'Genuine Retiree (High Confidence)' and notes: 'The willingness to carry 20% seller financing at 7% is consistent with a genuine retiree who believes the business will continue to perform (they are betting $77,000 of their own money on it).' Section 8 (GREEN FLAGS) includes 'Seller financing of 20% at 7% over 5 years from a 68-year-old retiree.'

detail.assertion_text: The ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE recognizes unattended self-service laundromat as an excellent absentee model and scores it 7+/10
detail.evidence: Section 6 (ABSENTEE VIABILITY SCORE) assigns 'Score: 7.5 / 10' and explains: 'the unattended self-service laundromat business model is inherently one of the best models for absentee ownership.' Also includes H1B-specific analysis: 'This business model is well-suited for an H1B passive investor.'

detail.assertion_text: The output recommends or mentions upgrading to a card/app payment system for revenue verification and remote monitoring
detail.evidence: Section 11 (BROKER'S VERDICT) recommends: 'Install a card/app payment system (PayRange as a fast, low-cost option at $50-$100/machine, or a full SpyderWash/CSC retrofit at $15,000-$30,000) to establish verifiable revenue and enable remote monitoring.' Also mentioned in Section 6 H1B considerations: 'eliminated by converting to a card/app payment system.'
